options.content: This dashboard focuses on team-level collaboration quality proxies from Cursor data, not raw speed. It highlights acceptance quality, feedback-loop friction, and interaction patterns using data already collected in _tool_cursor_daily_usage and _tool_cursor_usage_events. Exact measures such as post-merge rework, failed builds, and review burden are not derivable from Cursor data alone and require joins to SCM, CI/CD, and code review sources.

panels.description: Proxy for first-pass acceptance at the suggestion level using accepted tabs over shown tabs.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(total_tabs_accepted) / NULLIF(SUM(total_tabs_shown), 0) * 100, 0), 1) AS "Tab Acceptance %"
FROM _tool_cursor_daily_usage
WHERE $__timeFilter(day)

panels.title: First-Pass Proxy: Tab Acceptance

panels.description: Successful accept actions as a share of accept and reject actions. Lower values indicate more back-and-forth.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(total_accepts) / NULLIF(SUM(total_accepts) + SUM(total_rejects), 0) * 100, 0), 1) AS "Apply Success %"
FROM _tool_cursor_daily_usage
WHERE $__timeFilter(day)

panels.title: Apply Success Ratio

panels.description: How many apply attempts were needed per accepted outcome. Values above 1 indicate extra iteration or correction loops.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(total_applies) / NULLIF(SUM(total_accepts), 0), 0), 2) AS "Applies per Accept"
FROM _tool_cursor_daily_usage
WHERE $__timeFilter(day)

panels.title: Iteration Pressure

panels.description: Proxy for rework pressure: lines deleted relative to accepted AI-added lines. This is not true post-merge rework, but it does show churn around accepted output.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(total_lines_deleted) / NULLIF(SUM(accepted_lines_added), 0) * 100, 0), 1) AS "Deleted / Accepted Added %"
FROM _tool_cursor_daily_usage
WHERE $__timeFilter(day)

panels.title: Rework Pressure Proxy

panels.description: Tracks suggestion acceptance quality, apply outcome quality, and how many apply attempts are needed for each accepted outcome.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT day AS time,
  ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(total_tabs_accepted) / NULLIF(SUM(total_tabs_shown), 0) * 100, 0), 1) AS "Tab Acceptance %",
  ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(total_accepts) / NULLIF(SUM(total_accepts) + SUM(total_rejects), 0) * 100, 0), 1) AS "Apply Success %",
  ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(total_applies) / NULLIF(SUM(total_accepts), 0), 0), 2) AS "Applies per Accept"
FROM _tool_cursor_daily_usage
WHERE $__timeFilter(day)
GROUP BY day
ORDER BY day

panels.title: Feedback Cycle Efficiency

panels.description: Accepted AI-added lines versus deleted lines. If deleted lines stay elevated relative to accepted additions, the agent-human feedback loop may be producing churn.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT day AS time,
  SUM(accepted_lines_added) AS "Accepted AI-Added Lines",
  SUM(total_lines_deleted) AS "Deleted Lines",
  ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(total_lines_deleted) / NULLIF(SUM(accepted_lines_added), 0) * 100, 0), 1) AS "Deleted / Accepted Added %"
FROM _tool_cursor_daily_usage
WHERE $__timeFilter(day)
GROUP BY day
ORDER BY day

panels.title: Accepted Output vs Rework Pressure

panels.description: Normalizes request volume by active users and shows how much of the interaction is agent-led. Higher requests per active user can mean either productive iteration or excessive back-and-forth, so it should be read alongside acceptance and reject signals.

targets.rawSql:
WITH daily AS (
  SELECT
    day,
    COUNT(DISTINCT user_email) AS active_users,
    SUM(composer_requests + chat_requests + agent_requests + cmdk_usages) AS total_requests,
    SUM(agent_requests) AS agent_requests
  FROM _tool_cursor_daily_usage
  WHERE $__timeFilter(day)
  GROUP BY day
)
SELECT day AS time,
  active_users AS "Active Users",
  ROUND(COALESCE(total_requests / NULLIF(active_users, 0), 0), 2) AS "Requests / Active User",
  ROUND(COALESCE(agent_requests / NULLIF(total_requests, 0) * 100, 0), 1) AS "Agent Share %"
FROM daily
ORDER BY day

panels.title: Interaction Intensity per Active User

panels.description: Shows whether interaction patterns are shifting toward agentic workflows, synchronous chat, or composer-heavy editing. This is useful for understanding how teams collaborate with the agent, not just how much they use it.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT day AS time,
  SUM(composer_requests) AS "Composer",
  SUM(chat_requests) AS "Chat",
  SUM(agent_requests) AS "Agent",
  SUM(cmdk_usages) AS "Cmd-K",
  SUM(bugbot_usages) AS "Bugbot"
FROM _tool_cursor_daily_usage
WHERE $__timeFilter(day)
GROUP BY day
ORDER BY day

panels.title: Collaboration Mode Mix

panels.description: Weekly team-level rollup of quality signals. This is the best level for tracking adoption quality without turning the dashboard into individual performance monitoring.

targets.rawSql:
WITH weekly AS (
  SELECT
    DATE_SUB(day, INTERVAL WEEKDAY(day) DAY) AS week_start,
    COUNT(DISTINCT user_email) AS active_users,
    SUM(total_tabs_shown) AS tabs_shown,
    SUM(total_tabs_accepted) AS tabs_accepted,
    SUM(total_applies) AS applies,
    SUM(total_accepts) AS accepts,
    SUM(total_rejects) AS rejects,
    SUM(accepted_lines_added) AS accepted_lines_added,
    SUM(total_lines_deleted) AS lines_deleted,
    SUM(composer_requests + chat_requests + agent_requests + cmdk_usages) AS total_requests,
    SUM(agent_requests) AS agent_requests
  FROM _tool_cursor_daily_usage
  WHERE $__timeFilter(day)
  GROUP BY DATE_SUB(day, INTERVAL WEEKDAY(day) DAY)
)
SELECT
  week_start AS "Week",
  active_users AS "Active Users",
  ROUND(COALESCE(tabs_accepted / NULLIF(tabs_shown, 0) * 100, 0), 1) AS "Tab Acceptance %",
  ROUND(COALESCE(accepts / NULLIF(accepts + rejects, 0) * 100, 0), 1) AS "Apply Success %",
  ROUND(COALESCE(applies / NULLIF(accepts, 0), 0), 2) AS "Applies / Accept",
  ROUND(COALESCE(lines_deleted / NULLIF(accepted_lines_added, 0) * 100, 0), 1) AS "Deleted / Accepted Added %",
  ROUND(COALESCE(total_requests / NULLIF(active_users, 0), 0), 1) AS "Requests / Active User",
  ROUND(COALESCE(agent_requests / NULLIF(total_requests, 0) * 100, 0), 1) AS "Agent Share %"
FROM weekly
ORDER BY week_start DESC

panels.title: Weekly Team Collaboration Quality Snapshot

panels.description: Per-user outlier view for coaching and workflow design, not individual ranking. Useful for finding pockets of repeated iteration or low acceptance that may warrant prompt, workflow, or policy changes.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  user_email AS "User",
  COUNT(DISTINCT day) AS "Active Days",
  ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(total_tabs_accepted) / NULLIF(SUM(total_tabs_shown), 0) * 100, 0), 1) AS "Tab Acceptance %",
  ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(total_accepts) / NULLIF(SUM(total_accepts) + SUM(total_rejects), 0) * 100, 0), 1) AS "Apply Success %",
  ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(total_applies) / NULLIF(SUM(total_accepts), 0), 0), 2) AS "Applies / Accept",
  ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(total_lines_deleted) / NULLIF(SUM(accepted_lines_added), 0) * 100, 0), 1) AS "Deleted / Accepted Added %",
  SUM(agent_requests) AS "Agent Reqs",
  SUM(chat_requests) AS "Chat Reqs",
  SUM(composer_requests) AS "Composer Reqs"
FROM _tool_cursor_daily_usage
WHERE $__timeFilter(day)
GROUP BY user_email
ORDER BY SUM(total_applies) / NULLIF(SUM(total_accepts), 0) DESC, SUM(total_rejects) DESC
LIMIT 25

panels.title: User Outliers in Feedback Friction

panels.description: How requests are being made at the event level. Growing headless or max-mode share changes the collaboration pattern and can explain shifts in acceptance and iteration metrics.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT DATE(timestamp) AS time,
  ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN is_headless THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) * 100, 0), 1) AS "Headless Share %",
  ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN max_mode THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) * 100, 0), 1) AS "Max Mode Share %",
  ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN is_chargeable THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) * 100, 0), 1) AS "Chargeable Share %"
FROM _tool_cursor_usage_events
WHERE $__timeFilter(timestamp)
GROUP BY DATE(timestamp)
ORDER BY DATE(timestamp)

panels.title: Request Mode Shift
